headlines.title: Belgium say 'sense of injustice' spurred them on to knock US out of World Cup
headlines.description: Belgium's Nicolas Raskin says Fifa's decision to allow US striker Folarin Balogun to play against them fired them up for the last-16 tie in Seattle.

headlines.title: One million more UK homeowners set  to face higher mortgages
headlines.description: Homeowners face paying £45 a month more on average when they move onto a new deal over the next two years.

headlines.title: MI5 knew agent was misogynist 'obsessed' with violence, watchdog finds
headlines.description: A secret inquiry by the security service's watchdog concluded there were 'serious failings in MI5's management of Agent X’.

headlines.title: Endometriosis diagnosis set to be sped up with two new tests carried out by GPs
headlines.description: Experts hope they will be a game-changer and cut the nine-year or longer diagnosis waits patients can currently face.

headlines.title: Explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
headlines.description: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

headlines.title: The Odyssey stars wow red carpet at world premiere in London
headlines.description: Anne Hathaway, Zendaya, Tom Holland and Matt Damon were among the stars who walked the carpet in London for the premiere of Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Ancient Greek epic.

headlines.title: Harry begins UK visit as verdict due in privacy case against Daily Mail publisher
headlines.description: The Duke of Sussex begins a week of charity engagements and will hear the outcome of his newspaper court battle.

headlines.title: Zelensky to press Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
headlines.description: The Ukrainian president will use the Nato meeting in Turkey to argue he needs more interceptor missiles.

headlines.title: Jailers and officials at Russia's 'torture prisons' in Ukraine exposed by BBC
headlines.description: Former prisoners accuse the men of abuse in detention centres and want to see them brought to trial.

headlines.title: E-bike injury payouts top £110m and push up insurance premiums
headlines.description: It is only seven years since the first claim over an injury from a micromobility vehicle was made.

headlines.title: Report into mother and baby homes to be published
headlines.description: The report will contain a number of findings and recommendations, including on areas for an upcoming public inquiry to look into further.

headlines.title: The US World Cup dream ends in shambolic fashion - did red card saga play a part?
headlines.description: Mauricio Pochettino says he feels "disappointed with too many people" who brought "politics and manipulation" into the game as the US exit World Cup.

headlines.title: Watch: Belgium make light work of US to set up quarter-final tie with Spain
headlines.description: Belgium ease past a defensively sloppy USA in a match shrouded in controversy surrounding Folarin Balogun's delayed suspension, a scandal that made little impact on the pitch.

headlines.title: Ronaldo's World Cup career ends in tears after his sixth and final attempt to lift trophy
headlines.description: BBC Sport looks at the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career as he fails to win football's biggest prize.

headlines.title: Fans react to US loss after red card drama
headlines.description: The BBC's Nada Tawfik spoke to US fans after the FIFA World Cup co-hosts were knocked out of the tournament.

headlines.title: Watch: Spain into quarter-finals after Merino's late winner against Portugal
headlines.description: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.

headlines.title: 'I wear it on my middle finger': The rise of the defiant divorce ring
headlines.description: Around the world women are marking their split with new - or repurposed engagement - rings.

headlines.title: Resistance and revenge - Iran wanted to send a message with its farewell to Khamenei
headlines.description: Three days of public mourning in Tehran was a political spectacle Iran's leaders wanted the world to see, writes the BBC's international correspondent Lyse Doucet.

headlines.title: Meet Arthur Fery, the wildcard lifting the gloom around British players at Wimbledon
headlines.description: Seven days ago, there was doom and gloom around British players' performances at this year's Wimbledon. Then Arthur Fery stepped forward.

headlines.title: Can China repeat its EV success with robotaxis?
headlines.description: China's self-driving car firms have been given a headstart by the country's EV supply chain as they expand globally.

headlines.title: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
headlines.description: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

headlines.title: Erika Kirk comes face-to-face in court with husband's alleged killer
headlines.description: Charlie Kirk's widow was joined by the late US activist's family - all of whom attended court for the first time in the case.

headlines.title: Comparing student loans to phone contracts 'amounted to mis-selling', MPs say
headlines.description: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.

headlines.title: Women hit fertility tipping point at 49 - even with young donor eggs
headlines.description: Age-related changes in the womb lining may be the cause and could be treatable in the future, say experts.

headlines.title: Watch: Moment rare tornado lashes central Chinese cities
headlines.description: Videos shared with the BBC show debris flying through the air as the storm swept through Ezhou and Huanggang in Hubei province.

headlines.title: Banks accused of pushing customers away from basic accounts
headlines.description: Some of the UK's biggest banks have been failing their most vulnerable customers, according to the financial regulator.

headlines.title: Runner becomes fastest woman to complete the three peaks challenge on foot
headlines.description: Trish Patterson, 41, from Hythe is the fastest woman to complete the three peaks on foot.

headlines.title: Amazon bars breastfeeding boss from business course
headlines.description: Rachael Bews says she told on the way her child would not be allowed on site. Amazon has apologised for not communicating its policy clearly.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Is Trump interfering in the World Cup?
headlines.description: Trump spoke to Fifa president about controversial red card for USA player.

headlines.title: After 250 years, do US citizens still believe in the American Dream?
headlines.description: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

headlines.title: A shambolic end for the American dream - did Balogun saga play a part?
headlines.description: Mauricio Pochettino says he feels "disappointed with too many people" who brought "politics and manipulation" into the game as the US exit World Cup.

headlines.title: 'Overturn this' - Belgium taunt US and say Trump move fired them up
headlines.description: Belgium's Nicolas Raskin says Fifa's decision to allow US striker Folarin Balogun to play against them fired them up for the last-16 tie in Seattle.

headlines.title: Messi v Salah: A World Cup meeting of two national icons
headlines.description: Mohamed Salah and Lionel Messi will meet in the last 16 of the World Cup on Tuesday with their nations' hopes resting firmly on their shoulders.

headlines.title: 'Learn how to lose' - tempers flare in Wimbledon mixed doubles
headlines.description: Jelena Ostapenko and Marcelo Arevalo advance to the Wimbledon mixed doubles semi-finals in straight sets, but the victory is marred by a fiery post-match argument.

headlines.title: Ronaldo's World Cup career ends in tears as 'pandering' Martinez departs
headlines.description: BBC Sport looks at the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career as he fails to win football's biggest prize.

headlines.title: Giantkiller Fery stands tall to make history at Wimbledon for Britain
headlines.description: Seven days ago, there was doom and gloom around British players' performances at this year's Wimbledon. Then Arthur Fery stepped forward.

headlines.title: The man who invented a sport and played tennis with Willy Wonka
headlines.description: Brad Parks came up with the idea for a new sport while on a family picnic 50 years ago.
